commentaries.author: Hilary of Arles
commentaries.content: Why does James choose these two commandments as his examples? Because they are the ones which deal most closely with loving and with hating one's neighbors.

commentaries.author: Oecumenius
commentaries.content: James added these commandments in order to give examples of what he was talking about, which was love. For someone who loves his neighbors as he ought to will neither commit adultery with them nor kill them. When these things are done, they indicate contempt for the neighbor.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
